In [ ]:
# Importing required libraries
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import plotly.express as px
from google.colab import drive
from google.colab import files

from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score
from sklearn.cluster import AgglomerativeClustering, DBSCAN
from sklearn.metrics import pairwise_distances

from kneed import KneeLocator
from yellowbrick.cluster import KElbowVisualizer, SilhouetteVisualizer
from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import dendrogram, linkage
import hdbscan
In [47]:
# Load an CSV file
file_path = 'https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/1jml6i3whezn5dzarkb6a/credit_card_customers.csv?rlkey=o5yjpunceaa9vo51yyudn0qw8&st=6tcl8ivy&dl=1'
data = pd.read_csv(file_path)

In [44]:
#Now, we will check to see if we have any missing values across the dataset
missing_values = data.isna().sum().sort_values(ascending=False)
missing_summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "missing_count": missing_values,
    "missing_pct": (missing_values / len(data) * 100).round(2)
})
display(missing_summary[missing_summary["missing_count"] > 0])
duplicate_rows = data.duplicated().sum()
print("Number of duplicate rows:", duplicate_rows)
duplicate_ids = data["CLIENTNUM"].duplicated().sum()
print("Number of duplicate CLIENTNUM values:", duplicate_ids)
missing_count missing_pct 
Number of duplicate rows: 0
Number of duplicate CLIENTNUM values: 0
In [45]:
#Now, we will identify numeric versus categoric values
numeric_cols = data.select_dtypes(include=["int64", "float64"]).columns.tolist()
categorical_cols = data.select_dtypes(include=["object"]).columns.tolist()
print("Number of numeric columns:", len(numeric_cols))
print("Numeric columns:")
print(numeric_cols)
print("\nNumber of categorical columns:", len(categorical_cols))
print("Categorical columns:")
print(categorical_cols)

Number of numeric columns: 19
Numeric columns:
['CLIENTNUM', 'Customer_Age', 'Gender', 'Dependent_count', 'Education_Level', 'Marital_Status', 'Income_Category', 'Months_on_book', 'Total_Relationship_Count', 'Months_Inactive_12_mon', 'Contacts_Count_12_mon', 'Credit_Limit', 'Total_Revolving_Bal', 'Avg_Open_To_Buy', 'Total_Amt_Chng_Q4_Q1', 'Total_Trans_Amt', 'Total_Trans_Ct', 'Total_Ct_Chng_Q4_Q1', 'Avg_Utilization_Ratio']

Categorical columns:
['Attrition_Flag', 'Card_Category']
In [49]:
#Now, we will convert selected categorical variables into numeric codes, focusing especially on nominal data.
data ['Gender'] = data['Gender'].map({'M':0, 'F':1})
data ['Education_Level'] = data['Education_Level'].map({'Unknown':-1, 'Uneducated':0, 'High School':1, 'College':2, 'Graduate':3, 'Post-Graduate':4, 'Doctorate':4})
data ['Marital_Status'] = data['Marital_Status'].map({'Unknown':-1, 'Single':0, 'Married':1, 'Divorced':2})
data['Income_Category'] = data['Income_Category'].map({'Unknown':-1, 'Less than $40K':0, '$40K - $60K':1, '$60K - $80K':2, '$80K - $120K':3, '$120K +':4})

data

#Now, let's compute some summary statistics with our credit card data
numeric_summary = data[numeric_cols].describe().T
numeric_summary["median"] = data[numeric_cols].median()
numeric_summary["std"] = data[numeric_cols].std()
numeric_summary["pct_zero"] = (data[numeric_cols].eq(0).mean() * 100).round(2)
display(numeric_summary.sort_values("pct_zero", ascending=False))

In [51]:
#Knowing that we have many numeric values, let's begin visualizing them
import math
cols_to_plot = numeric_cols.copy()
n_cols = 4
n_rows = math.ceil(len(cols_to_plot) / n_cols)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, n_cols, figsize=(16, 4 * n_rows))
axes = axes.flatten()
for i, col in enumerate(cols_to_plot):
    axes[i].hist(data[col], bins=30)
    axes[i].set_title(col, fontsize=10)
    axes[i].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)
for j in range(i + 1, len(axes)): #turn offf some
    axes[j].axis("off")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [52]:
#Knowing that "CLIENTUM" is the ID, let's do correlation analysis for our numeric variables
numeric_for_corr = [col for col in numeric_cols if col != "CLIENTNUM"] #remove
corr_matrix = data[numeric_for_corr].corr()
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 10))
plt.imshow(corr_matrix, aspect="auto")
plt.colorbar()
plt.xticks(range(len(numeric_for_corr)), numeric_for_corr, rotation=90)
plt.yticks(range(len(numeric_for_corr)), numeric_for_corr)
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap of Numeric Features")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
corr_pairs = (
    corr_matrix.abs()
    .where(np.triu(np.ones(corr_matrix.shape), k=1).astype(bool))
    .stack()
    .sort_values(ascending=False)
)
display(corr_pairs.head(15))

In [53]:
# Now that we see the correlations, we can do the final feature selection for clustering. We will remove highly correlated variables & redundant features, only keeping the variables capturing different types of customer behavior
excluded_features = [
    "CLIENTNUM",
    "Avg_Open_To_Buy",
    "Total_Trans_Amt",
    "Customer_Age"
]
cluster_features = [
    col for col in numeric_cols
    if col not in excluded_features
]
print("Excluded features:")
print(excluded_features)

print("\nFinal numeric features for clustering:")
print(cluster_features)
display(data[cluster_features].describe().T)

Excluded features:
['CLIENTNUM', 'Avg_Open_To_Buy', 'Total_Trans_Amt', 'Customer_Age']

In [54]:
#Now that we have our target variables. We need to do feature scaling so that our values are ready for various clustering methods.
X = data[cluster_features].copy()
scaler = StandardScaler()
X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X)
X_scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(
    X_scaled,
    columns=cluster_features,
    index=data.index
)
display(X_scaled_df.describe().T) #verify

K-Means Clustering¶

In [ ]:

Hierarchical Clustering¶

In [ ]:

DBSCAN¶

In [ ]:
# Initialize HDBSCAN model
hdbscan_model = hdbscan.HDBSCAN(
    min_cluster_size=5,
    min_samples=3,
    cluster_selection_method="eom"
)

# Fit model and assign cluster labels
X_scaled_df["HDBSCAN_Cluster"] = hdbscan_model.fit_predict(
    X_scaled_df[cluster_features]
)

# Inspect results
print(X_scaled_df["HDBSCAN_Cluster"].value_counts())

HDBSCAN_Cluster
 7    6093
-1    3997
 4       6
 2       6
 6       5
 1       5
 0       5
 5       5
 3       5
Name: count, dtype: int64
In [ ]:
noise_ratio = (X_scaled_df["HDBSCAN_Cluster"] == -1).mean()
print(f"Noise ratio: {noise_ratio:.2%}")

Noise ratio: 39.47%
In [ ]:
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

pca = PCA(n_components=2)
X_pca = pca.fit_transform(X_scaled_df[cluster_features])

data['PCA1'] = X_pca[:, 0]
data['PCA2'] = X_pca[:, 1]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.scatterplot(
    x=data['PCA1'],
    y=data['PCA2'],
    hue=data['HDBSCAN_Cluster'],
    palette='Set2'
)
plt.title("HDBSCAN Clustering (PCA Projection)")
plt.show()

In [25]:
clean_clusters = data[data['HDBSCAN_Cluster'] != -1]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.scatterplot(
    x=clean_clusters['PCA1'],
    y=clean_clusters['PCA2'],
    hue=clean_clusters['HDBSCAN_Cluster'],
    palette='Set2'
)
plt.title("HDBSCAN Clustering with Noise Removed")
plt.show()
In [28]:
# Fit HDBSCAN model
hdbscan_model = hdbscan.HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=100, gen_min_span_tree=True)
X_scaled_df['HDBSCAN_Cluster'] = hdbscan_model.fit_predict(X_scaled_df[['Total_Trans_Ct', 'Avg_Utilization_Ratio']])
In [27]:
# Visualize HDBSCAN Clustering Results
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.scatterplot(x=X_scaled_df['Total_Trans_Ct'], y=X_scaled_df['Avg_Utilization_Ratio'], hue=X_scaled_df['HDBSCAN_Cluster'], palette='Set2')
plt.title("HDBSCAN Clustering")
plt.show()
In [29]:
# Visualize HDBSCAN Clustering Results without Noise
clean_clusters = X_scaled_df[X_scaled_df['HDBSCAN_Cluster'] != -1]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.scatterplot(x=clean_clusters['Total_Trans_Ct'], y=clean_clusters['Avg_Utilization_Ratio'], hue=clean_clusters['HDBSCAN_Cluster'], palette='Set2')
plt.title("HDBSCAN Clustering with Noise Removed")
plt.show()
In [30]:
# Plot the condensed tree
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))
hdbscan_model.condensed_tree_.plot(select_clusters=True, label_clusters=True, cmap='viridis')
plt.title("HDBSCAN Condensed Tree")
plt.show()
In [31]:
# Summary statistics by cluster
# First, add the HDBSCAN_Cluster column to the original data DataFrame
data['HDBSCAN_Cluster'] = X_scaled_df['HDBSCAN_Cluster']

cluster_summary = data.groupby('HDBSCAN_Cluster').agg(
    count=('HDBSCAN_Cluster', 'size'),
    age_mean=('Customer_Age', 'mean'), # Using existing 'Customer_Age' column
    spending_mean=('Total_Trans_Amt', 'mean') # Using existing 'Total_Trans_Amt' column for spending
)

cluster_summary

# Plot age and spending by cluster (removed income plot as no numeric income column)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 6)) # Adjusted for 2 plots

# Plot average age by cluster
sns.barplot(x=cluster_summary.index, y=cluster_summary['age_mean'], ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title('Average Customer Age by Cluster')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Cluster')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Average Customer Age')

# Plot average spending by cluster
sns.barplot(x=cluster_summary.index, y=cluster_summary['spending_mean'], ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title('Average Total Transaction Amount by Cluster')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Cluster')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Average Total Transaction Amount')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
